description:
A VeraCrypt encrypted volume at /home/ga/Volumes/corrupted_volume.hc has suffered primary header corruption (simulating a disk error). The volume contains three critical files: project_ssh_config, infrastructure_hosts, and quarterly_metrics.csv.

Volume parameters:
- Password: RecoverMe2024!
- Encryption: AES
- Hash: SHA-512
- PIM: 0 (default)
- No keyfiles

The primary header is damaged, so a normal mount will fail. You must mount the volume using VeraCrypt's embedded backup header feature. In the CLI, this is the --backup-header flag. In the GUI, this is the 'Use backup header embedded in a volume if available' option in Mount Options.

Your tasks:
1. Mount the corrupted volume at /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1 using the embedded backup header
2. Verify the three files are accessible inside the mounted volume
3. Compute SHA-256 checksums of all three files
4. Create a recovery report at /home/ga/recovery_report.txt with the following format:
   - First line: RECOVERY REPORT
   - One line per file: <sha256_checksum>  <filename>
   - Last line: RECOVERY COMPLETE: 3 files verified

The checksum lines must use the exact format output by sha256sum (two spaces between hash and filename, filename only without path).
